title: What causes a person to turn into LCL
display_title: What causes a person to turn into LCL
wikitext:
[]: What causes a person to turn into LCL?             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Sat Mar 23, 2013 1:59 pm Something I found odd about the assumption on the wiki that soul removal causes a person to turn into LCL is that it postulates a number of things: 1) The body is only being hold together by the AT field, like a water balloon 2) The soul produces the AT field 3) Removing the soul from the body turns off the AT Field, like a water balloon exploding 4) Angels (and Rei) are composed of exotic matter that retains it shape but can be manipulated by the AT Field, like clay to the human water balloon The problem with this is that 1) Angels will sometimes wholly or partially disintegrate when their cores break, 2) Rei begins literally falling apart when her AT field starts breaking down, 3) severed limbs do not turn into LCL, and 4) Kyoko Sohryu doesn't turn into LCL after having her soul removed. This doesn't make much sense to me. EDIT: I found an explanation someone wrote , which states simply "AT Fields can exist independently of a soul" like some kind of fifth fundamental force or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Mar 23, 2013 2:31 pm 1) Angel matter is Angel matter, we don't know how it works or if it even works consistently 2/3) While analogical to a "water balloon" it doesn't necessarily have to be connected within 3d space, AT fields have been projected outside of the physical body. So there's sort of this "self image" type of thing, and that includes limbs that you've lost because it's still sort of your limb. 4) Kyoko's soul was split, part of it was in Eva-02 and part of it was still in her body. See: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Classified_Information#The_Second_Children Also, more ATF discussion here: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/5200/AT-Field-Technicalities/

[]:             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Sat Mar 23, 2013 5:01 pm Ornette wrote: 2/3) While analogical to a "water balloon" it doesn't necessarily have to be connected within 3d space, AT fields have been projected outside of the physical body. So there's sort of this "self image" type of thing, and that includes limbs that you've lost because it's still sort of your limb.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sat Mar 23, 2013 5:06 pm Well from what I understand Aristotle thought of the psyche, the soul or spirit, as being intristic to a being, its very essence. He thought it impossible for something to lack an essence. Maybe the AT field works sort of the like that. When someone dies, their essence changes. But if the soul/essence is simply torn away, there is nothing there: it returns to blankness.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sat Mar 23, 2013 5:40 pm Zenoseiya wrote: Then you culture that cell into a limb or an organ. Why does it not turn into LCL? Does it have a soul? Why?

[]:             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Sat Mar 23, 2013 7:05 pm arael wrote: Well, in the NGE world, in theory that would not be possible, without an AT field then the body would collapse;

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sat Mar 23, 2013 11:06 pm Zenoseiya wrote: According to what? The point of this topic is to put forward the idea that AT fields can exist independently of souls.

[]:             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Sun Mar 24, 2013 9:55 am arael wrote: Well, it is explicitly said that a beings generate AT fields; hence, no being=no AT field, being with no soul=tang... Although the soul of a deceased can be obtained after it's death, so it is also implied that the soul resides the body some time after death...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Mar 24, 2013 1:26 pm Zenoseiya wrote: So because they aren't mentioned, they can be assumed not to exist? Then the world of NGE shouldn't even remotely resemble our own.

[]: Re: What causes a person to turn into LCL?             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 24, 2013 1:55 pm Zenoseiya wrote: Something I found odd about the assumption on the wiki that soul removal causes a person to turn into LCL is that it postulates a number of things:

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Mar 24, 2013 2:07 pm The explanation that postulates the least amount of extraneous entities is that AT fields can exist without souls.

[]:             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Sun Mar 24, 2013 10:39 pm Azathoth wrote: It kind of doesn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Mar 25, 2013 12:33 am Or the AT-Fields of Lilith-based life are just different. We don't have any textual evidence from the show that AT-Fields are constantly keeping tangification at bay, only that a disruption of the AT-Field under a specific circumstances (when it's sufficiently spread out, as happened with Yui, Kyoko and Shinji, or with an anti-AT-Field) causes the matter of the body to instantly revert LCL. Honestly, thinking about this too hard is a waste of time. People turning into puddles of orange liquid is absurd, unless the fluid that Lilith secretes also contains calcuium, iron, phosphorous, and other stuff that doesn't make a lick of sense. Terrestrial life is made up of the Earth; an exobiotic origin makes sense, but not in the way NGE presents it. All life on Earth being made out of goop that dribbled out of an alien is ridiculous. It probably does contain iron, though- it's repeatedly said to taste/smell like blood, and blood smells and tastes the way it does partly because of its iron content.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Mar 25, 2013 1:06 am Zenoseiya wrote: Can you provide a citation on that second part? I find it a bit hard to believe that Rei reappeared after dying only because her soul was magically extracted from her vaporized corpse. How does that even work?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Mar 25, 2013 3:42 pm Why do shed skin, hair, nail clippings, feces, sweat, tears, spit, snot, and all the other organic muck human beings produce not turn into LCL after it leaves contact with the body? Why do insects sealed in amber not turn into LCL, despite being entombed for millions of years? You can't seriously expect me to belief a person's own residual self-image magically keeps those things from turning into LCL an arbitrarily long period of time after their death, do you?

[]:             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Sun Mar 31, 2013 3:37 pm arael wrote: We have the perfect example of Asuka's mom, where her soul split in the contact experiment and then had the 2 parts reunited in Eva 02 after her death (soul recovered from light bulb-hanging corpse)...

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sun Mar 31, 2013 4:38 pm Zenoseiya wrote: It's never stated nor implied that her soul is extracted from her corpse by NERV, and there is no reason why they would need to since half a soul functions just as well a two halves. ... Rei was incinerated ! There was no body to extract a soul from!

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Mar 31, 2013 5:13 pm Zenoseiya wrote: It's never stated nor implied that her soul is extracted from her corpse by NERV, and there is no reason why they would need to since half a soul functions just as well a two halves.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Apr 01, 2013 3:06 pm Staying in the corpse for millions of years doesn't make sense. Where is that implied? There's no indication of that anywhere I can see. In fact, the very opposite: there is only a limited amount of souls, which came directly from Chamber of Guf or were created by splitting other souls (somehow... is there a wheel of reincarnation or something that does that? the explanation makes less and less sense you more you think about it); if souls are not being recycled in a timely manner, then something bad will probably happen since souls apparently serve a vital role in life.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 01, 2013 3:25 pm arael wrote: Maybe it does, maybe an Eva with half a soul goes homicidal once in a while (like Eva 00) and they couldn't afford the production model to do that...
